disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Possible pregnancy (reproductive-age female, pregnancy status unconfirmed) — a pregnancy test is mandatory to exclude ectopic pregnancy; screen for unilateral/lateralised pain, shoulder-tip pain, syncope, abnormal bleeding
trigger_question_examples: Could you be pregnant, and have you done a pregnancy test?
Is the pain building on one particular side, and any shoulder-tip pain, faintness, or unusual (not typical period) bleeding?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I'm not sure if I could be pregnant and haven't tested; the pain is central and cramping, no one-sided or shoulder pain, and the bleeding is my normal period.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No features of appendicitis/torsion/PID — no migratory right-lower-quadrant or sudden severe lateralised pain, no fever, no abnormal/offensive discharge, no localising peritoneal features
trigger_question_examples: Has the pain moved to or become sharp/constant on the right lower side, and any fever or offensive discharge?
Was there a sudden severe one-sided pelvic pain (as with a twisted ovary)?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, it's central cramping tied to my period, not sharp on one side, no fever and no unusual discharge.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Cyclical pain beginning ~6–12 months after menarche, with the period, relieved by NSAIDs, no deep dyspareunia/non-cyclical pain — primary (not secondary) dysmenorrhoea; plus dehydration from vomiting
trigger_question_examples: Did this pain start within a year or two of your first period and come only with your periods, easing with anti-inflammatories?
How much have you kept down and are you passing urine?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yes, it started within a year of my first period and comes with my periods; I've kept almost nothing down and haven't passed much urine.
